operating_state: Operating revenue increased 94% to $13.8 billion driven by passenger revenue recovery as travel demand strengthened through the quarter with strong summer season initiation. System capacity reached 82% of 2019 levels with domestic business travel continuing improvement and international revenue recovering to 80% of 2019 levels as travel restrictions eased. Operating income reached $1.5 billion, improving $703 million year-over-year despite higher fuel costs and volume-related expense increases from operational restoration.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 95% to $12.3 billion primarily from $1.7 billion fuel expense increase and volume-related costs as operations scaled. Fuel expense rose 41% compared to 2019 levels representing the primary margin pressure offset by improved yield performance. Operating margin compressed versus 2019 despite revenue recovery due to elevated fuel costs and operational rebuilding expenses.
material_events: Board elected Leslie D. Hale and Greg Creed as directors in April with committee appointments to Finance, Safety & Security, Audit, and Personnel & Compensation committees. Annual shareholder meeting in June approved all fourteen director nominees and executive compensation while rejecting lobbying disclosure proposal. CEO Ed Bastian presented at Bernstein Strategic Decisions Conference in June.
macro_exposures: No material changes to risk factors were disclosed during the quarter beyond those previously identified in the 2021 Form 10-K filing.
forward_outlook: Management expects system capacity recovery to reach 83% to 85% of September 2019 levels in the September 2022 quarter with continued improvement throughout 2022. International revenue recovery is expected to continue trailing domestic revenue through 2022 despite recent policy changes and improved advance sales trends.
uncertainty_profile: Recovery trajectory remains dependent on sustained travel demand improvement and fuel cost volatility management. International business travel recovery timing remains uncertain despite improved advance sales trends led by Atlantic region performance.
